# Lock paths relative to a git worktree's toplevel canonicalize identically across
# every worktree of the same repo, so two agents each in their own worktree checkout
# contend for the very same logical file instead of two unrelated absolute paths
# that happen to share a name.
env BREEZE_DIR=$WORK/state

exec git init -q repo
cd repo
exec git -c user.email=test@example.com -c user.name=test commit -q --allow-empty -m init
exec git worktree add -q ../wt -b wt1branch
mkdir a/b
cd a/b

exec breeze lock acquire file.txt --as alice --ttl 5m --json
stdout '"paths":\s*\[\s*"a/b/file.txt"\s*\]'

cd $WORK/wt
exec git rev-parse --show-toplevel

# The same logical path, named relative to THIS worktree's own toplevel, must
# conflict with alice's still-held lock even though this is a different directory.
! exec breeze lock acquire a/b/file.txt --as bob --ttl 5m
stderr 'conflict|lock'

exec breeze lock list --json
stdout '"paths":\s*\[\s*"a/b/file.txt"\s*\]'
stdout '"holder":\s*"alice"'

exec breeze stop
